title: lichen_l2_input may leak net_pkt if net_pkt_write partially succeeds then fails
description:
Location: lichen_l2.c:687-693

**What goes wrong:** net_pkt_write() can fail partway through copying ipv6_len bytes. The code correctly calls net_pkt_unref() on failure, but the pkt may have partial data written. If net_recv_data() is ever changed to accept packets with NET_PKT_DATA_ACCESS_CONTIGUOUS requirement, or if the IPv6 stack processes partial headers, this could cause issues.

Actually on re-reading: net_pkt_unref() is called, so there's no leak. The partial data is freed with the packet. This is not a bug.

Retracting.
close_reason: Not a bug - net_pkt_unref properly frees the packet including any partial data
